abstract: In recent years, the field of artificial intelligence has undergone a profound paradigm shift driven by the emergence of generative models capable of high-level performance in both supervised and unsupervised learning contexts. Generative AI has established new benchmarks for addressing complex real-world challenges across diverse domains, including medical diagnostics, image-to-image translation, natural language processing, and multimodal data fusion. This paper provides a systematic review and analysis of contemporary advancements in Generative AI, offering a detailed exploration of architectural innovations and application-specific models. The study highlights the transformative impact of large language models on text generation and investigates the success of generative techniques in synthesizing high-fidelity visual and interdisciplinary content. A primary contribution of this work is the coherent synthesis of recent breakthroughs—ranging from Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) to Transformers and Diffusion models—while mapping the future trajectory of the field. Finally, the paper addresses the critical necessity of Responsible AI, discussing the ethical considerations and sustainability principles essential for the ongoing development and ethical deployment of generative technologies.
